title: Daily Briefing — May 04
headline: Tech rips, breadth stretches, but financials lag: Flows favour patience over chase.
body: Right, so markets are still riding momentum — SPY pushing $720.65 with a daily close well above the 20 MA ($698.50), and Tech leading the pack (XLK RS=107.9, Mom=101.3, price $161.87). Fear & Greed is at 66.1, up from the panic lows last month (23.7) but not yet in the danger zone; sentiment is heating up, but hasn't boiled over.

Sector rotation is textbook: XLK and XLRE are firmly in the 'Leading' quadrant, with XLK’s price and Real Estate (XLRE) running ahead of their MAs. Meanwhile, Energy (XLE $58.85) and Industrials (XLI $172.96) are weakening, Financials (XLF $51.92) are high-risk and lagging AGAIN — the canary stays muted. Healthcare and Discretionary show some improvement but still carry elevated risk.

Contrarian lens: Retail is chasing hard (look at AAPL $280.14, AMZN $268.26 — both stretched above their 20/50/200 MAs), but we’re not at extreme greed—yet. VIX is normal (18.11), so volatility doesn’t shout ‘danger.’ Still, financials lag, pair ratios are drifting down, and with the weekly mean reversion zone way below, patience rules: abundance mindset, not FOMO. If it’s in the press, it’s probably in the price.
callout_body:
SPY: 20 MA=$698.50, 50 MA=$679.48
XLK: $161.87 (approaching), 20 MA=$151.66
XLF: $51.92, SMA200=$52.30 (watch for 200 MA test)
AAPL: $280.14, 20 MA=$266.36
NVDA: $198.45, 20 MA=$197.22
Financials lagging: High risk, no breakouts
Fear & Greed: 66.1 — elevated but not extreme
action_items: Watch Financials (XLF $51.92) for breakdowns — reduction in equity risk if it loses SMA200 ($52.30)
Stick to weekly mean reversion zones; do NOT chase stretched leaders like AAPL or AMZN above their 20/50/200 MAs
closing_quote: Less trades = more wins. Patience, react, don’t predict.
